# openapi-java-client

ART Logistik exchange

- API version: 

- Build date: 2026-03-04T15:59:00.699066900+03:00[Europe/Moscow]

- Generator version: 7.7.0

API description


*Automatically generated by the [OpenAPI Generator](https://openapi-generator.tech)*

## Requirements

Building the API client library requires:

1. Java 17+
2. Maven/Gradle

## Installation

To install the API client library to your local Maven repository, simply execute:

```shell
mvn clean install
```

To deploy it to a remote Maven repository instead, configure the settings of the repository and execute:

```shell
mvn clean deploy
```

Refer to the [OSSRH Guide](http://central.sonatype.org/pages/ossrh-guide.html) for more information.

### Maven users

Add this dependency to your project's POM:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.openapitools</groupId>
  <artifactId>openapi-java-client</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
  <scope>compile</scope>
</dependency>
```

### Gradle users

Add this dependency to your project's build file:

```groovy
  repositories {
    mavenCentral()     // Needed if the 'openapi-java-client' jar has been published to maven central.
    mavenLocal()       // Needed if the 'openapi-java-client' jar has been published to the local maven repo.
  }

  dependencies {
     implementation "org.openapitools:openapi-java-client:1.0.0"
  }
```

### Others

At first generate the JAR by executing:

```shell
mvn clean package
```

Then manually install the following JARs:

- `target/openapi-java-client-1.0.0.jar`
- `target/lib/*.jar`

## Getting Started

Please follow the [installation](#installation) instruction and execute the following Java code:

```java

import ru.arvato.api.generated.*;
import ru.arvato.api.generated.auth.*;
import ru.arvato.api.generated.model.*;
import ru.arvato.api.generated.api.CreateRequestApi;

public class CreateRequestApiExample {

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        ApiClient defaultClient = Configuration.getDefaultApiClient();
        defaultClient.setBasePath("https://api-prod.arvato.ru");
        
        // Configure HTTP bearer authorization: jwt
        HttpBearerAuth jwt = (HttpBearerAuth) defaultClient.getAuthentication("jwt");
        jwt.setBearerToken("BEARER TOKEN");

        CreateRequestApi apiInstance = new CreateRequestApi(defaultClient);
        Services service = Services.fromValue("STOCKSTATE"); // Services | Type of data to export
        String feedbackUrl = "http://localhost"; // String | Optional URL to send response
        OffsetDateTime dateFrom = OffsetDateTime.now(); // OffsetDateTime | Period start date in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss format (or short yyyy-MM-dd to API requests)
        OffsetDateTime dateTo = OffsetDateTime.now(); // OffsetDateTime | Period finish date in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss format (or short yyyy-MM-dd to API requests)
        String invoiceNr = "invoiceNr_example"; // String | Invoice number for PRODUCT_RECEIVE_CONFIRMATION, MOVEMENTS, PRODUCT_OUTGO_CONFIRMATION requests
        String orderNr = "orderNr_example"; // String | Customer order number
        SalesChannel salesChannel = SalesChannel.fromValue("site"); // SalesChannel | Sales channel (site or marketplace name) for STOCKSTATE_CHANNEL, ORDERSTATE requests
        try {
            String result = apiInstance.pushRequest(service, feedbackUrl, dateFrom, dateTo, invoiceNr, orderNr, salesChannel);
            System.out.println(result);
        } catch (ApiException e) {
            System.err.println("Exception when calling CreateRequestApi#pushRequest");
            System.err.println("Status code: " + e.getCode());
            System.err.println("Reason: " + e.getResponseBody());
            System.err.println("Response headers: " + e.getResponseHeaders());
            e.printStackTrace();
        }
    }
}

```

## Recommendation

It's recommended to create an instance of `ApiClient` per thread in a multithreaded environment to avoid any potential issues.
